notes: Converted from the old chapter extraction schema.
Hidden facts, motives, and faction pressures require living-world re-extraction.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

The seed package primarily describes public appearances of various Shang clan members at an auction. Events like Fang Yuan obtaining a box with a token, Shang Qiuniu appearing, Shang Chaofeng and Shang Fuxi arriving together, Shang Pulao/Shang Suanni/Shang Bixi entering, and Shang Xin Ci/Shang Chiwen arriving are all publicly observable events. No private knowledge, secret motives/plans, or future-known information is directly supported by the source pointers. The relations about alliances (Shang Chaofeng & Shang Fuxi being allies, Shang Pulao/Shang Suanni/Shang Bixi forming a small alliance) are derived from narrative description of their public behavior (walking together, sitting together) but the source does not directly state these as known facts by any specific character. Shang Xin Ci being a private child of Shang Yanfei with maternal clan Zhang is mentioned in the relations but the source pointer only shows her name, not the private fact itself. No hidden/player-visibility fact candidate can be confidently extracted from this seed.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

Seed package primarily contains appearance events and entity introductions at a拍卖会 (auction). Characters appear but no meaningful action with clear observed motive is shown. Events are mostly 'appearance' type (arriving at auction). No character performs an action from which a cautious one-step inferred motive can be derived without psychological leaps. Relations describe alliances but no observed behavior supporting motive inference.
Merged second-stage living-world enrichment; still requires expert/manual review.

insufficient source-backed seed: The seed primarily describes appearances of various Shang clan young masters at an auction, with faction relations noted (Ju family defecting to Shang, alliances among young masters). However, no concrete pressure, threat, interest, or resource stake is established from the seed facts. The Ju family defecting to Shang (ptr not directly in pool for that event) is mentioned but lacks actionable pressure context. The alliances among Shang young masters are noted but no active pressure or conflict is depicted. No faction-level pressure can be confidently extracted from this seed alone.
